description: The Communist Party of Iran (CPI) was founded on 2 September 1983 through the merger of the [Komala Party of Iranian Kurdistan](/orgs/komala-party-of-iranian-kurdistan) with several Iranian leftist organizations, including the Union of Communist Militants and a faction of the [Peykar Organization](/orgs/peykar-organization). [Mansoor Hekmat](/people/mansoor-hekmat) was a co-founder and leading theoretician of the party. The CPI opposed both the Islamic Republic and the Soviet model of socialism, advocating worker-council communism. In 1991, Hekmat and a significant portion of the membership left to form the [Worker-communist Party of Iran](/orgs/worker-communist-party-of-iran). In 2000, the [Komala](/orgs/komala-party-of-iranian-kurdistan) wing under [Abdullah Mohtadi](/people/abdullah-mohtadi) also split, further weakening the party. The CPI continues to operate in exile.
editorial_notes: Founded 1983 from Komala + other groups. Significantly weakened by 1991 and 2000 splits.
